ingredients.item: ¾ cup (180 ml) cold heavy whipping cream
ingredients.original: ¾ cup (180 ml) cold heavy whipping cream

ingredients.item: ¾ cup (170 g) short-grain rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely
ingredients.original: ¾ cup (170 g) short-grain rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely

ingredients.item: 1 Add the milk to a medium saucepan and heat over medium heat until hot but not boiling. Remove from the heat.
ingredients.original: 1 Add the milk to a medium saucepan and heat over medium heat until hot but not boiling. Remove from the heat.

ingredients.item: 2 In a small bowl, combine the cream and cornstarch and mix until the cornstarch has dissolved. Using a mortar and pestle, crush the mastic gum (if using) into a fine powder.
ingredients.original: 2 In a small bowl, combine the cream and cornstarch and mix until the cornstarch has dissolved. Using a mortar and pestle, crush the mastic gum (if using) into a fine powder.

ingredients.item: 3 Add the rice to a large pan or skillet at least 2 inches (5 cm) deep. Add 2 ladles of the hot milk and gently simmer together over medium heat, stirring constantly. When the rice has absorbed most of the milk, add a few more ladles of hot milk and continue to stir until most of the milk is absorbed. Continue adding milk, a few ladles at a time, stirring until most of it is absorbed. (This method is similar to how you would cook a risotto; while slow, this process will yield very creamy results.) Once you have used all the milk, add the sugar and stir for a few minutes until it is dissolved. Add the whipping cream–cornstarch mixture and continue to stir until the pudding is thickened and the rice is cooked to a very soft consistency. This process should take 30 to 40 minutes in total.
ingredients.original: 3 Add the rice to a large pan or skillet at least 2 inches (5 cm) deep. Add 2 ladles of the hot milk and gently simmer together over medium heat, stirring constantly. When the rice has absorbed most of the milk, add a few more ladles of hot milk and continue to stir until most of the milk is absorbed. Continue adding milk, a few ladles at a time, stirring until most of it is absorbed. (This method is similar to how you would cook a risotto; while slow, this process will yield very creamy results.) Once you have used all the milk, add the sugar and stir for a few minutes until it is dissolved. Add the whipping cream–cornstarch mixture and continue to stir until the pudding is thickened and the rice is cooked to a very soft consistency. This process should take 30 to 40 minutes in total.

ingredients.item: 4 Stir in the mastic gum powder (if using) or other flavoring (see Notes). Remove the pan from the heat. Pour the rice pudding into oven-safe 5- to 6-ounce (150 to 180 ml) ramekins for individual servings, arranging them on a large sheet pan. Turn on the oven broiler and place the sheet pan directly under the broiler. Broil for 1 to 2 minutes (at the most), until the surfaces are caramelized and look spotty and browned. Remove and let cool for a few minutes.
ingredients.original: 4 Stir in the mastic gum powder (if using) or other flavoring (see Notes). Remove the pan from the heat. Pour the rice pudding into oven-safe 5- to 6-ounce (150 to 180 ml) ramekins for individual servings, arranging them on a large sheet pan. Turn on the oven broiler and place the sheet pan directly under the broiler. Broil for 1 to 2 minutes (at the most), until the surfaces are caramelized and look spotty and browned. Remove and let cool for a few minutes.

ingredients.item: 5 Serve warm (my favorite), at room temperature, or cover with plastic wrap, refrigerate for 2 hours, and then serve cold.
ingredients.original: 5 Serve warm (my favorite), at room temperature, or cover with plastic wrap, refrigerate for 2 hours, and then serve cold.
